stories.title: Immigration and Asylum Bill: Second Reading Opening Speech
stories.summary: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood delivered the opening speech for the second reading of the Immigration and Asylum Bill, setting out the government's case for major reform of the UK's asylum system.

stories.title: UK Bans Support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
stories.summary: The UK government has designated the IRGC as a terrorist organisation under the National Security Act, making support for the group unlawful. The IRGC, Russia's GRU Volunteer Corps, and the Islamic Movement of Companions of the Right are among the first groups listed.

stories.title: Immigration Bill Amendment to Allow Removal of Rochdale Abuse Convicted Citizens
stories.summary: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood is tabling an amendment to the Immigration Bill that would make it possible to deport Commonwealth citizens convicted of serious criminality, including those involved in the Rochdale abuse scandal.

stories.title: UK Sanctions Iran-Linked Criminal Group Behind Attacks on Jewish Locations
stories.summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office has announced UK sanctions against an Iran-linked criminal group that publicly claimed responsibility for recent attacks on Jewish locations in the UK.

stories.title: Biggest Pension Reforms: Value for Money Framework Timeline Laid Out
stories.summary: The DWP has set out the timeline for the biggest pension reforms in a generation, including a new Value for Money framework that will require pension schemes to measure and publish performance on investment outcomes and service quality — described as 'top of the league or in the relegation zone'.

stories.title: £60 Million Fund Launched to Transform Employment Support for Disabled People
stories.summary: DWP has launched a £60 million fund inviting businesses, charities and organisations to bid for funding to develop innovative ideas to support disabled people move closer to and into employment.

stories.title: Government Publishes 30by30 Delivery Plan with £37 Million/Year Funding
stories.summary: DEFRA has published the UK's 30by30 Delivery Plan, backed by up to £37 million a year over the next three years to accelerate progress toward protecting 30% of land for nature by 2030. The plan also covers volunteer programmes to support biodiversity.

stories.title: Land Use Framework Publications Released
stories.summary: DEFRA published a collection of products and publications developed through the Land Use Framework, outlining how land across England can be best used to balance food production, environmental goals and rural economic needs.

stories.title: £15 Billion Funding Boost: At Least Six New Air Defence Warships Announced
stories.summary: The MoD has announced at least six new air defence warships to defend UK waters and boost domestic shipbuilding, as part of a £15 billion new funding package to transform the Armed Forces. The announcement also includes high-speed boats and new drones for elite Commando units.

stories.title: 35 Universities Partner in New Defence Universities Alliance
stories.summary: A new Defence Universities Alliance has been launched with 35 UK universities to strengthen defence research, build critical skills and support the UK's future defence capabilities.

stories.title: MoD Requires All Industry Partners to Achieve Cyber Certification by December 2026
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has set a December 2026 deadline for all industry partners to achieve Level 0 of the Defence Cyber Certification (DCC), as part of its strategy to build cyber resilience across the UK's defence supply chain.

stories.title: Landmark UK-Switzerland Services Trade Deal Unveiled — £5.2 Billion Annually
stories.summary: The UK has sealed an unprecedented services trade agreement with Switzerland, estimated to increase UK exports to Switzerland by £5.2 billion annually. The deal also simplifies exporting processes through paperless trading and paves the way for easier travel for British citizens.

stories.title: Barnsley Tech Town's £800,000 AI Skills Fund Opens for Applications
stories.summary: DSIT has announced that applications are open for the AI Upskilling Challenge Fund in Barnsley, inviting organisations to deliver innovative AI training to help local workers, businesses and communities benefit from artificial intelligence.

stories.title: New Research on Children's Circumvention Behaviours Online Published
stories.summary: DSIT has published new research commissioned from BMG Research examining how children aged 11 to 17 experience and circumvent age verification and parental controls online, to inform online safety policy.

stories.title: Oxford Researchers Develop Vaccine for New Ebolavirus Variant — Fast-Track Review Underway
stories.summary: Researchers at the University of Oxford announced on 13 July 2026 they have developed a vaccine for a new variant of Ebolavirus. The NHS Health Research Authority (HRA) has initiated a fast-track review of the vaccine.
